title: Binding the same container port to >1 host port is OK
body:
The initial version of the new port code mistakenly restricted this, so un-restrict it. We still need to maintain the map of container ports, unfortunately (need to verify if the port in question is a duplicate, for example).

Fixes #7062

It may be better to store all the host ports a container port is bound to - it will use more memory (potentially significantly more) but otherwise we risk duplicating ports.

changes LGTM, one question/suggestion for tests that can be ignored if I'm off base.  
Tests aren't happy, but IDK if that's expected atm.
